summary: Iteration 3 validation: (1) granular permission system (18 perms / 7 groups), (2) Excel xlsx exports for products/stock/sales/productions. Created /app/backend/tests/test_permissions_excel.py with 22 pytest cases — ALL 22 PASS. Re-ran iteration 1+2 regression suite (48 tests) — all still pass (total 70/70 backend). Frontend smoke: admin login → /settings → İzinler dialog opens with exactly 7 grouped sections and 18 permission checkboxes + 7 group-toggle buttons (data-testid='toggle-group-<key>'), save works. Excel buttons on /products, /reports, /production, /stock all download valid .xlsx files (PK ZIP magic, size 5-6 KB, correct filenames urunler.xlsx / stok-raporu.xlsx / uretim.xlsx / satislar.xlsx). Found and fixed two render-time bugs in Sales.jsx that broke the page completely for all users.

ui_bugs.issue: Page crashed at render with two ReferenceErrors: (a) `FileSpreadsheet is not defined` — icon used in Excel button but not imported from lucide-react; (b) `canCreate is not defined` — used to gate 'Yeni Satış' button but never declared. Also `downloadXlsx` from ../lib/permissions and `useAuth` from ../context/AuthContext were not imported. Pattern from Products.jsx was not replicated in Sales.jsx during the iteration-3 refactor. Fixed by adding missing imports + `const { user } = useAuth(); const canCreate = hasPermission(user, 'sales.create'); const canDelete = hasPermission(user, 'sales.delete');`. After fix, Sales page renders and Excel download works.

issues: Date range filters still use browser-native <input type='date'> (mm/dd/yyyy placeholder) instead of shadcn Calendar/Popover. Pre-existing from iteration 2 — still not addressed.

action_items: Sales.jsx render-time crash has been patched by testing agent (missing imports + canCreate/canDelete declaration). Please review the diff at /app/frontend/src/pages/Sales.jsx lines 2 and 11-19 — the fix follows the same pattern used in Products.jsx. Apply `canDelete` gating to row delete actions (currently not wired into the row rendering — only declared). Also gate row click that opens receipt by `sales.view` if needed.
Verify other pages (Customers.jsx) for similar missed iteration-3 setup — Customers.jsx was not exercised in this run beyond auth/me check.
critical_code_review_comments: Permission system implementation is clean: require_permission() helper bypasses for admin, falls back to default_permissions(role) when user.permissions is None, and returns 403 with the specific perm key in detail ('Yetkisiz erişim (products.delete)') which matches frontend expectation.
PUT /api/users/{uid} correctly validates permissions list — rejects unknown keys with 400. Only allows mutating ['name', 'role', 'active', 'permissions'] — safe.
Excel _build_xlsx is shared and consistent (header style, money/number formatting, freeze pane). _xlsx_response uses correct MIME + Content-Disposition.
/api/export/* endpoints correctly gate by perm (products.view, reports.view, sales.view, productions.view). Confirmed 401 unauth and 403 when view perm stripped.

server.py is now 1293 lines — flagged in earlier iterations, growing further. Strongly recommend splitting into routers (/auth, /users, /products, /sales, /reports/exports) before next feature iteration.

Sales.jsx iteration-3 refactor was incomplete (see CRITICAL bug above). Suggest a pre-commit grep for `canCreate|canEdit|canDelete|downloadXlsx|hasPermission` to ensure imports match usage across all pages.
GET /api/permissions does not require any specific permission — exposes all permission keys to any authenticated user (acceptable for UX but consider gating by users.manage if treated as sensitive).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows after Sales.jsx fix (Settings permission dialog, Products/Reports/Production/Stock/Sales Excel downloads)

test_credentials: admin@stoktakip.com / Admin123! (re-seeded on every backend startup). Temp users (perm_personel_*@example.com, perm_rapor_*@example.com, reg_def_*@example.com) created and cleaned up in pytest fixtures.
seed_data_creation: test_permissions_excel.py creates 2 module-scoped temp users (one personel, one rapor) and several short-lived products for permission-gated CRUD checks. Cleanup performed via DELETE /api/users/{id} in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 endpoints validated: GET /api/permissions (18 perms / 7 groups), GET /api/auth/me returns 'permissions' array, POST /api/auth/register populates permissions field with role defaults, PUT /api/users/{id} accepts and validates permissions array (400 on unknown key), require_permission() returns 403 with detail 'Yetkisiz erişim (<perm>)'. Excel endpoints: /api/export/{products,stock,sales,productions}.xlsx — all return application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet, content starts b'PK', size ~5-6KB, gated by *.view perm (products.view / reports.view / sales.view / productions.view). Frontend: Settings.jsx has data-testid='edit-perms-<userId>' to open dialog, data-testid='perm-<permKey>' on each checkbox label, data-testid='toggle-group-<groupKey>' for group toggles, data-testid='save-perms-btn' to save. Excel buttons: data-testid='export-products-xlsx', 'export-sales-xlsx', 'export-stock-report-xlsx'; Production and Stock pages also have Excel buttons (no testid yet, selected via button:has-text('Excel')). Sales.jsx Sales() now uses useAuth + hasPermission(user,'sales.create'/'sales.delete') — pattern to verify elsewhere.
rca of the issue: Sales.jsx crash RCA: Main agent's iteration-3 refactor added Excel button (line 131-138 using FileSpreadsheet icon + downloadXlsx()) and permission-gated 'Yeni Satış' button (line 139 `{canCreate && ...}`) but did NOT update the import block at lines 1-13 nor declare `canCreate` at the top of the Sales() component. Identical patches were correctly applied to Products/Production/Stock/Reports — Sales was missed. Reproduction: login as admin → click 'Günlük Satış' sidebar entry → 'Uncaught runtime errors: FileSpreadsheet is not defined' overlay appears; after that import added, second error 'canCreate is not defined' surfaces. Mitigation applied: (1) added `FileSpreadsheet` to lucide-react import on line 2, (2) added `import { hasPermission, downloadXlsx } from '../lib/permissions'` and `import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext'` after format import, (3) added `const { user } = useAuth(); const canCreate = hasPermission(user,'sales.create'); const canDelete = hasPermission(user,'sales.delete');` at start of Sales() component body. Verified Sales page renders, sales list displays, and Excel download produces a valid 5.5 KB xlsx. NOTE: `canDelete` is declared but main agent should still wire it into the per-row delete button (currently the cancel-sale flow appears row-based via receipt dialog — verify whether sales.delete gating is needed there).
